subject: Sociology
title: Gramsci and Cultural Hegemony
description: A micro-lesson that makes Gramsci and Cultural Hegemony usable.

hook.text: The ruling class doesn't just control armies. It controls ideas. And that's the more powerful weapon.

buildup.text: Gramsci argued that dominant groups maintain power through hegemony — making their worldview seem like common sense.

discovery.text: Media, schools, and religion spread ruling-class ideas so effectively that the oppressed accept them as natural.

twist.text: Hegemony is never total. Counter-hegemonic movements challenge dominant ideas — feminism, civil rights, labor unions.

climax.text: Power doesn't just rely on force. It relies on consent — getting people to believe the system is fair.

punchline.text: The strongest chains are the ones you can't see.

quiz.question: What is cultural hegemony?
options: Dominant groups making their worldview seem like common sense
